verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonical cytosolic HSP70 chaperone with no membrane anchor or TM domain in its baseline state. However, heat-shock proteins including HSP70 are well-documented to translocate to the cell surface under stress conditions (heat shock, ER stress, oxidative stress, hypoxia) and in tumor cells, where they are recognized by immune receptors and serve as damage-associated molecular patterns (DAMPs). Additionally, HSP70 is released via non-classical secretion and exosomes, and documented to undergo cell-surface display on stressed and dying cells. Baseline compartment is cytosolic / organellar, but stress-induced and cancer-cell ecto-display qualify as cell_state_induced surface biology.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The extent and functional relevance of HSP70 surface display under baseline vs. stress conditions; baseline is predominantly cytosolic but stress-induced ecto-presentation is well-documented in heat shock, cancer, and cell-death contexts. Whether stress-induced display is stable enough or frequent enough to count toward clinical targeting remains context-dependent.
